H5_answer_leaked_in_hint.description: The correct answer text appears somewhere inside the hint layers. A child (or parent) reading the hint sees the answer directly.
H5_answer_leaked_in_hint.fix: Rewrite hints to guide thinking without revealing the exact answer value. Use 'think about what happens when you add the ones column' not '3+4=7'.
H5_answer_leaked_in_hint.priority: P0 — this defeats the purpose of hints entirely

H3_explicit_spoiler.description: Hint literally says 'The answer is X' — most egregious spoiler pattern.
H3_explicit_spoiler.fix: Delete 'The answer is...' and replace with Socratic guidance. These are the easiest to find-and-fix with a script.

H10_last_layer_computes_answer.description: The final hint layer shows the full computation (e.g., '4 × 11 = 44 cm'). Per Gate 3, the last hint must leave ONE step for the child to do themselves.
H10_last_layer_computes_answer.fix: Change last layer to show the setup ('4 × 11 = ?') without the result, or show a visual model.

summary.clean_hints: ~7,500 questions have no detected hint issues

W9_no_reinforce_micro_question.description: The 3-R Framework requires a micro-question (R3: Reinforce) in the 'Why?' explanation — e.g., 'Can you try again with borrowing?' Only 11.2% of questions have this.
W9_no_reinforce_micro_question.fix: Append a micro-question to each diagnostic. Can be templated: 'What do you get if you [operation] [numbers]?'

W7_no_recontextualize.description: R1 (Re-Contextualize) means stating the error neutrally — 'You chose 36, which would be correct if...' Only 13.7% do this.
W7_no_recontextualize.fix: Prepend option-specific context: 'If you chose [X], you may have [misconception].'

W8_no_redirect.description: R2 (Redirect) offers a mental model — 'Remember, when borrowing...' or 'Think about place value.' 23.7% have this.
W8_no_redirect.fix: Add a redirect sentence per misconception. Best done with LLM few-shot generation.

W10_no_misconception_name.description: Diagnostics don't name the specific misconception (e.g., 'subtracted smaller from larger in each column'). Many just say 'Wrong formula' without detail.
W10_no_misconception_name.fix: Map each distractor to a named misconception from the misconception taxonomy.

W2_incomplete_option_coverage.description: Fewer diagnostics than wrong options — some wrong choices have no 'Why?' explanation at all.
W2_incomplete_option_coverage.fix: Generate missing diagnostics for uncovered options.

items: Remove all 'The answer is X' spoiler patterns from hints (4,001 questions) — regex find-and-replace
Remove direct computation from last hint layer (2,751 questions) — truncate at '=' sign
Fix answer leaks where hint contains exact correct_answer text — rewrite hint to use conceptual language
Generate 3-R compliant Why? explanations for all 22,467 questions using few-shot LLM pipeline
Template: R1='You chose {option}. This would be right if {misconception_context}.' R2='Remember: {mental_model}.' R3='Can you try: {micro_question}?'
Generate named misconception IDs for all 16,654 questions missing them
Fill missing diagnostic coverage (2,400 questions with incomplete options)
Rewrite remaining ~11,964 answer-leaking hints to follow 3-Layer Scaffolding protocol
Layer 1: Cognitive nudge (concept reminder, no numbers)
Layer 2: Strategic bridge (method hint, one option fades in UI)
Layer 3: Visual scaffolding or worked example (50/50 in UI), but leave final step to child
LLM few-shot with quality rubric scoring (≥4 average across layers)
